title: SQL Injection
summary: SQL Injection occurs when untrusted input is included directly in SQL queries without proper parameterization.
safe_fix: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements.

title: Use of Hard-coded Credentials
summary: Hard-coded credentials expose secrets directly in source code and can allow unauthorized access.
safe_fix: Store secrets in environment variables or a secure secrets manager.

title: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm
summary: Weak cryptographic algorithms can expose sensitive data or make passwords easier to crack.
safe_fix: Use modern approved cryptographic libraries and password hashing algorithms.

title: Missing Authentication for Critical Function
summary: Critical functionality without authentication can allow unauthorized users to access protected actions.

title: Improper Access Control
summary: Improper access control allows users to perform actions or access resources without proper authorization.
safe_fix: Enforce authorization checks based on user identity, role, and permissions.

title: Active Debug Code
summary: Debug code left enabled in production can expose sensitive information and internal application behavior.
safe_fix: Disable debug mode and remove development-only behavior in production.

title: Exposure of Sensitive Information
summary: Sensitive information may be exposed through errors, debug output, or unsafe configuration.
safe_fix: Disable verbose errors and avoid exposing sensitive internal details.
